DOMAIN: general
TASK: {'domain': 'film_restoration', 'task': "Define scope for the digital restoration of 200 nitrate-era films (1910-1951) from a national film archive's vault, where 30% have active vinegar syndrome (acetic acid off-gassing causing autocatalytic base degradation), 15% exist only as duplication negatives with generation-loss artifacts, and 12 titles have no surviving original negative and must be reconstructed from multiple incomplete prints held by 4 different archives across 3 countries. Estimate effort for each restoration tier (preservation scan only, basic digital restoration, full 4K restoration with manual frame-by-frame repair), accounting for the variable scanning speed (acetate base vs nitrate base handling protocols), the specialized color grading expertise required for 2-strip Technicolor processes, and the intellectual property clearance process for 40 orphan works with uncertain rights holders. Build the schedule with film vault cold-storage cycling constraints (only 20 cans/day can be acclimated from 5°C vault to scanning suite temperature), scanner maintenance windows, and a grant funding disbursement schedule with milestone deliverables every 6 months."}

task_reasoner.response:
Domain: film_restoration
Task: Define scope, estimate effort tiers, and build a schedule for digital restoration of 200 nitrate-era films with varying conditions.
Steps:
1. Inventory & Condition Assessment – Compile a master list of all 200 titles, noting vinegar syndrome status (30%), duplication negatives (15%), missing originals (12), Technicolor 2-strip presence, and orphan work flags. Result: Detailed condition matrix for prioritization.
2. Tier Definition – Establish three restoration tiers:
   • Preservation Scan Only
   • Basic Digital Restoration (color correction, noise reduction)
   • Full 4K Restoration with Manual Frame‑by‑Frame Repair
   Result: Clear criteria and deliverables per tier.
3. Scanning Speed Calibration – Determine scanning throughput for acetate vs nitrate bases, accounting for handling protocols and safety windows. Estimate average minutes per reel per tier. Result: Time budget per film per tier.
4. Color Grading Expertise Allocation – Identify films requiring 2‑strip Technicolor grading; assign specialists and schedule additional hours (≈1.5× baseline). Result: Extra labor cost and time estimates.
5. Intellectual Property Clearance – Map 40 orphan works, initiate rights holder outreach, document responses, and allocate legal review periods (~3 weeks per title). Result: IP clearance timeline and contingency buffer.
6. Cold‑Storage Cycling Constraints – Only 20 cans/day can be acclimated from 5°C vault to scanning suite. Compute daily throughput limits per tier; schedule acclimation windows accordingly. Result: Daily film intake plan.
7. Scanner Maintenance Windows – Incorporate routine maintenance (every 200 hours of use) and unexpected downtime into the calendar. Result: Downtime buffer in schedule.
8. Effort Estimation – Combine scanning time, grading, manual repair, and IP clearance to produce effort hours per title per tier. Aggregate for all 200 titles. Result: Total man‑hours and cost forecast.
9. Grant Funding Disbursement & Milestones – Align deliverables with 6‑month grant milestones (e.g., 25% of preservation scans completed, 50% of basic restorations finished). Tie budget releases to milestone approvals. Result: Cash flow schedule.
10. Final Schedule Construction – Integrate all constraints into a Gantt‑style timeline, ensuring no overlap beyond available resources and respecting vault cycling limits. Result: Comprehensive project plan ready for stakeholder review.

Deliverables:
- Tiered effort estimates (hours, cost)
- Detailed daily intake & acclimation schedule
- IP clearance status tracker
- 6‑month milestone budget release chart
- Full Gantt timeline with scanner maintenance and cold‑storage windows
task_reasoner.internal_reasoning: I broke down the complex restoration workflow into discrete, logically ordered steps: inventory, tier definition, technical constraints (scanning speed, color grading), legal clearance, physical handling limits, resource scheduling, effort estimation, funding alignment, and final timeline synthesis. Each step is justified by project requirements and leads to a concrete output that feeds into the next stage.

Estimate effort for each restoration tier (preservation scan only, basic digital restoration, full 4K restoration with manual frame-by-frame repair), accounting for the variable scanning speed (acetate base vs nitrate base handling protocols), the specialized color grading expertise required for 2-strip Technicolor processes, and the intellectual property clearance process for 40 orphan works with uncertain rights holders. Build the schedule with film vault cold-storage cycling constraints (only 20 cans/day can be acclimated from 5°C vault to scanning suite temperature), scanner maintenance windows, and a grant funding disbursement schedule with milestone deliverables every 6 months.
